items.name: The Shadowkin
items.body:
The Shadowkin, who call themselves the Nargálid in their own old tongue, are forged in shadow and kept alive by twilight. Tall and slender, they wear skin of pure void, black as the space between stars, and their eyes and mouths glow with an unsettling white light fixed in a permanent open smile, whether they feel joy, grief, or fury.

Gaunt and eerie as they look, the Shadowkin are no one's monsters. They are thinkers, watchers, and survivors of a world where light is a rare and dangerous thing.

They come from Shadakar, a realm locked in everlasting dusk. Leafless forests sway in silence, rivers glow faintly with a kind of bioluminescent sorrow, and mountains rise like teeth under a deep indigo sky that never once changes. It is a land of reflection, hidden war, and whispered memory.
items.roleplay: Shadowkin are often reserved, strategic, and inwardly focused. Their unsettling appearance belies a deep well of loyalty and empathy, though these must be earned. Their relationship with shadow is more than metaphorical. It is part of who they are.

items.name: The Ironclad
items.body:
The Ironclad are sentient suits of enchanted armor, awakened leftovers of an age that already ended. Their makers vanished long ago. The armor rose anyway, off battlefields and out of broken halls, each spirit bound to its plate and to some purpose it can no longer fully name. Centuries of wear have made them distinct, etched over with runes, scars, and symbols nobody alive still knows how to read. They come in every build, from towering juggernauts to lean tactical frames, and they all share one fact. There is no flesh under there.

They hail from Mireleor, a shattered realm once rich with arcane technology and now ground down under the behemoths that still roam its plains. The sky churns, the earth shifts, and ancient ruins lie scattered across the land like bones. The Ironclad pick their way through all of it with a resolve that does not flinch, hunting for some remnant of where they came from and what they were for.
items.roleplay: Ironclad are stoic, driven, and duty-bound. While their affinity for magic is weak, many seek to understand it, often fumbling through arcane practice with curiosity or frustration. They revere the ruins of their creators, guarding them or searching for answers in the dust.

items.name: The Fribbit
items.body:
The Fribbit are agile, amphibious folk born of rain, mud, and war. Smooth vivid skin, long expressive tongues, and a grace that carries them through the dense jungles of Mireth, a world pulsing with magic, danger, and divine flora. Outsiders take them for playful or peculiar. Underneath that is a warrior people shaped by discipline, resilience, and hard spiritual training.

Skin color carries real weight in their culture. Darker-toned Fribbits are usually raised toward stealth and close combat, brighter ones toward magic, alchemy, or ranged work.

Their bodies are built for leaping, for reading the lay of a place at a glance, and for tongues that handle nearly any task, though almost never in a fight. There, a Fribbit's tongue is considered far too valuable to risk.
items.roleplay: Fribbits are curious and alert, always scanning for movement, opportunity, or the perfect place to leap. Their society is communal but deeply proud, and Fribbits are often driven by legacy, spiritual conviction, or a desire to prove themselves in a world that misunderstands them.

items.name: The Wolfir
items.body:
The Wolfir are humanoid lupines, muscular and fur-covered, shaped by the cold and tempered by a code. They stand upright on powerful limbs that end in clawed hands and feet, with piercing eyes and fangs that mark heritage as much as biology.

They come from Vulkaris, a world of endless snow and biting wind, where the Wolfir learned early that survival means unity. Their society runs on discipline, respect, and pack loyalty, in that order.

A Wolfir lives a deep contradiction, a seeker of peace who stays ready for violence at all times. They hold hard to honor, tradition, and protection, and when something pushes them past that, they answer with a ferocity few can match. Some live and die inside the pack. Others walk alone, carrying the storm in silence.
items.roleplay: Wolfir are loyal and proud, often viewing their allies as packmates. They respond best to strength, clarity, and trust, and expect the same in return. Every action is weighed against duty, every word carries weight.

items.name: The Foxel
items.body:
The Foxel are elegant, fox-like folk from a world where magic moves like water. Slender and quick, draped in fur that holds a faint glow, they are tuned to the wild arcana of Wildermire, a realm of floating isles, sentient trees, and glowing lakes where every root hides a secret and every breeze carries a little enchantment.

A Foxel keeps to itself by preference, happier with its own thoughts and the song of the leyline winds than with the noise of a crowded town. In Wildermire it navigates by instinct, following currents of energy nobody else can see.

They rarely pick a fight, and they are genuinely dangerous when cornered, all misdirection and speed and spellcraft. Most are more trickster than warrior. A lot of them bury their pain under a laugh. All of them are watching far more than they let on.
items.roleplay: Foxel are clever, curious, and laced with mischief. They are drawn to puzzles, magic, and rare phenomena, but they protect what is theirs with cunning and fire.

items.name: The Thurani
items.body:
The Thurani are towering beastfolk, horned and furred and shaped for war. Their bodies speak to raw strength, and their faces carry the scars of having survived plenty of it. Half humanoid and half animal, they are defined by endurance rather than savagery, whatever outsiders assume. Many have been enslaved as gladiators, mercenaries, or warbeasts, and they remain a proud, ancient people still fighting to take their legacy back.

They come from Therion, a world worn down by endless conflict, barren plains under jagged mountains, the magical fallout of forgotten wars still simmering in the soil beneath elemental storms.

Their fur runs in shades of coal and earth, their horns curve like a goat's, and long beards trail windswept behind them. The yellow or green eyes hold more than instinct. There is deep thought in them, and a fair amount of bitter memory.
items.roleplay: Thurani are shaped by hardship. They honor strength but respect cunning. They are quick to fight, but only for something worth bleeding for. Their loyalty runs deeper than blood and their pride is not easily broken.

items.name: The Dwellers
items.body:
The Dwellers are the adaptable heartbeat of the Sphere. They look the most human of any lineage, but their real strength was never in blood or bone. It is in their knack for enduring, adapting, and changing to fit whatever the world throws at them. Born across countless worlds, they carry the legacy of lands still thriving and lands long fallen.

They hail from everywhere. The quiet valleys of Valoria, the mist-drenched forests of Hirayama, the cracked glass deserts of Pyranthia, the frostbitten reaches of Norska, and a hundred places past those. No two Dwellers share an origin, and yet they all share the one trait that matters, which is that they are still here.

Physically they come in every shape there is, tall and short, lean and broad, colored and customed by wherever they were raised. A Dweller does not always stand out in a room. They always belong in it.
items.roleplay: Dwellers are pragmatic thinkers and curious explorers. Whether bold or cautious, they are driven by the need to understand and overcome. Their vast range of histories allows them to adapt socially, culturally, and strategically to almost any situation.

items.name: The Lunarian
items.body:
The Lunarians carry twilight in them, all grace and quiet resilience. Gray-skinned, with eyes that shimmer in shades of teal, they bring a calming presence into a room. Slender, poised, and usually a little enigmatic, a Lunarian looks like they are holding a secret and waiting to decide whether you get to hear it.

They come from Sellenia, a realm of unending moonlight where silver rivers run through glowing forests and glassy lakes, all of it under a huge moon that never wanes and never lets the hush break.

Most Lunarians end up far from home, threading themselves into distant worlds on purpose. They are masters of the quiet kind of influence, the sort you find in circles of power, of art, of arcane knowledge. The moon is a long way off, but they carry its calm certainty wherever they go.
items.roleplay: Lunarians are introspective, intelligent, and deeply attuned to the rhythms of magic and nature. While often reserved, they are fiercely loyal to those who earn their trust. They are nightwalkers, truth-seekers, and steady forces in a chaotic world.

items.name: The Sprittle
items.body:
The Sprittle are tiny quicksilver beings of leaf, bark, bloom, and breeze, a few inches tall and made of equal parts whimsy and wildness. Whether they are flitting through a glade on gossamer wings or darting between dew-soaked roots, they live in time with the endless song of Verdantia.

Verdantia runs on Courts of the seasons. Summer meadows wrapped in sunlight, Winter groves laced with frost, stormy lakes that ripple when Spring arrives, and Autumn woods burning gold with rich decay.

A Sprittle's skin takes after wherever it lives, mossy green or petal pink or bark brown or misty gray, and its hair might pass for flower petals, feathers, or morning frost. The eyes always gleam with wonder, and always with a little bit of trouble.
items.roleplay: Sprittle are joyous, chaotic, and deeply loyal to those they trust. They are natural tricksters, but never heartless. To them, mischief is a dance and a way to test, teach, and discover. They are as much caretakers of the land as they are agents of unpredictability.

items.name: The Arboril
items.body:
The Arboril are living trees. Towering, sentient, grown from bark, leaf, and root, they move with the slow certainty of things that have outlasted whole kingdoms, their limbs creaking and their voices rolling deep through the canopy.

They come from Sylvanoth, a realm of vast trees, glowing groves, and wind that never stops whispering. Nature runs the place unopposed, and the Arboril serve as its stewards and its shepherds. Each one is born of a particular tree, oak, ash, birch, redwood, and it shows in the grain of their bark-skin, the leaves in their hair, and the color of their eyes.

They live on sunlight and company, and they loathe metal the way you would loathe a blade made from someone's bone. Even so, when war comes for the wild places, an Arboril will pick up a forged weapon and use it.
items.roleplay: Arboril are slow to act but unstoppable once roused. They are gentle giants who would rather plant than fight. But when the forest calls, they answer with fury and storm.

items.name: The Rekindled
items.body:
The Rekindled are the dead who got up again. Undead forms that shuffle, stride, or stagger through the wreckage of who they used to be, animated by forbidden rituals that lash soul back to bone. Some are bare skeletal husks, some are still rotting, and all of them burn with a spark that will not quite go out.

They are summoned into Mortavia, a cursed and apocalyptic realm where wild sorcery roams loose and death is only the opening move. Chaos rules the broken lands, and the Rekindled walk the ruins of kingdoms nobody remembers, carrying purposes that came back to them in pieces.

Each one keeps faint echoes of an older life. Their speech is arcane, felt more than heard, carried on magical resonance. They can be grotesque to look at, and a great many of them are still noble, fierce, or kind underneath it.
items.roleplay: The Rekindled straddle two worlds: the memory of life and the reality of undeath. They may be seeking redemption, vengeance, or simply trying to understand why they still exist.

items.name: The Vyrathe
items.body:
The Vyrathe are aristocrats of shadow, all eerie refinement and whispered hunger. Porcelain skin, vibrant eyes, long black hair, and a bearing that walks the line between elegance and dread. They feed to control rather than to survive, drawing lifeforce out of others and doling fragments of it back to whoever they mean to bind.

They emerge from Vyrathis, a twilight land under crimson moons, its mist-laced forests dotted with crumbling fortresses that still whisper their old secrets. In Vyrathis the sun never truly rises.

The Vyrathe keep to themselves. They disdain kinship and family ties and hold their distance even from allies. When the power from feeding overflows, a Vyrathe's body changes, the beautiful facade giving way to the darker, uglier truth of a soul bound up in ancient ritual oaths.
items.roleplay: The Vyrathe are masterful predators in social and physical spaces alike. Cold, intelligent, and always several steps ahead, they trust rarely and never forget a slight.

items.name: The Capling
items.body:
The Caplings are mushroom-born warriors, stewards of the spore who hold blade and broth in equal regard. They move like water, keep silent on purpose, and hand out their loyalty only after you have earned it through fire and fermentation both.

They come from Sporion, a damp realm of fungal towers, whispering vents, and mycelial networks that pulse underfoot. The land breathes out spores in slow luminous spirals that drift across toadstool glades and hollow roots, and the Caplings build their lives there out of ritual and rhythm, martial discipline braided into culinary art.

Caplings grow their offspring from their own spores, cultivating the next sprout themselves. Each one comes up a little different in spirit while carrying the same discipline as the one before it.
items.roleplay: Caplings are graceful, focused, and deeply attuned to cycles of growth and decay. They speak little, act with purpose, and find joy in the quiet rituals of preparation, whether in combat or the kitchen.

items.name: The Hallowed
items.body:
The Hallowed are celestial-born, radiant and unbending in their virtue, with bodies that look carved from marble and eyes that gleam with a clarity that unsettles people. Some say they were stars once, given form. Others say they are the leftovers of fallen gods, still trying to earn their light back.

They come from Elysara, a realm of harmony and near-perfection where isles of light drift through sapphire skies and the rivers sing with their own resonance. There, compassion is not a virtue so much as the basic culture.

For all their physical power, the Hallowed use it with restraint, favoring their fists and finding honor in directness and disgrace in cruelty. They also, genuinely, cannot lie. Not a polite cannot, an actual one. So when a Hallowed speaks, the honesty can be devastating.
items.roleplay: The Hallowed are kind without naivety, strong without arrogance, and honest without exception. They often seek meaning beyond themselves and quests to protect, restore, or illuminate.

items.name: The Elken
items.body:
The Elken are woodland folk, soft-spoken and sharp-eyed and tuned to the quiet harmony of wild places. They move through the forest like whispers, talk to trees as if to old friends, and tend wounded beasts with patient hands. Harming anything without cause is simply foreign to them. Protecting it, though, they do with a quiet precision that catches people off guard.

Their homeland is Thaloryn, a realm of living greenery where the Elken build nothing from stone. They live in woven hollows, cliff-nest groves, and burrows shaped out of vine and wind.

They are lithe and graceful, hair wild as bramble and eyes deep and earnest, and they eat no meat, living on leaves and roots and fruit. None of which should be mistaken for softness. Threaten their home and an Elken will have a bow strung before you have finished the sentence.
items.roleplay: Elken are gentle but brave, peacekeepers who value harmony over conflict, yet will not hesitate to defend the balance of life when pushed.

items.name: The Spireborn
items.body:
The Spireborn are elemental incarnates, humanoids shaped out of the raw forces of the world. Each one is born from an ancient monolith, a spire, and carries the essence of its homeland in its body and its soul. They are earth, wind, fire, and water made into something that walks, speaks, and defends with the world's own fury.

They rise from Oroniel, a primordial realm where each towering spire pulses with old magic. It is from those spires that the Spireborn come, bound for good to the land that forged them.

A Spireborn sculpts its own shape, usually after the terrain of its birth spire, so the outer shell might read as polished stone, churning smoke, crackling flame, or falling water. Under that shell the pattern holds: a heart of fire, blood of water, bones of stone, and a soul carried on the wind.
items.roleplay: Spireborn are proud and territorial, elemental forces given purpose and form. Their personalities often reflect their elemental expression, and their duty to their homeland is unwavering.

items.name: The Harfolk
items.bonus: Permanent extra lucky die for Treasure Rolls
items.body:
The Harfolk are energetic, fur-covered folk with rabbit features, twitchy curiosity, and a pace almost nobody can keep up with. Keen ears, toothy grins, and a bottomless appetite for company make them endlessly social and just as endlessly mischievous.

They come from Burhallow, a world of rolling green hills, lush groves, and sprawling underground hovel-cities lit by glowing tunnels. Harfolk get around by sound and instinct, and they fill their lives with joy, feasts, and the occasional dirt-skidding getaway.

The easygoing act hides a people built to survive. Those big ears catch the softest footstep, and those teeth chew through root, rope, or a well-stocked pantry with equal ease. A Harfolk is swift, reactive, and very hard to catch, especially with mischief on the line.
items.roleplay: Harfolk are the spark of any group: playful, impulsive, but fiercely community-minded. They bring levity to darkness and loyalty to chaos.

items.name: The Harrowed
items.body:
The Harrowed were not born into a people. They were thrown out of one. Every Harrowed is a former Hallowed, a celestial stripped of grace and hurled through the Brim, the portal out of Elysara into Brimsever, for sins the Hallowed judged past forgiving.

Where a Hallowed wears a halo as proof of virtue, the Harrowed lost theirs in the fall. What is left is a pair of shattered horns, jagged and dark, the broken stubs of what they used to be. Some arrive in Brimsever with their eyes or mouths sewn shut, a last verdict from the ones who cast them out, meant to seal away whatever guilt might otherwise leak into the world.

Brimsever itself was broken by cosmic malice, torn open by a ritual that reached past the Skyvault, and its air still churns with volatile rifts and infernal wind. The Harrowed do not belong there. They simply survive it. No two wear their ruin the same way, but they all carry the same marks: pale ashen skin, haunted eyes, broken horns, and a weight that trails them like a second shadow.
items.roleplay: The Harrowed are fallen celestials: driven, scarred, and often desperate to understand what they did to deserve what was done to them.

items.name: The Shrewish
items.body:
The Shrewish are clever, whiskered folk built around one idea: survive, comfortably if at all possible. Big eyes for spotting an opening, incisors strong enough to gnaw through nearly anything, and a knack for scavenging, building, and planning that has no real equal. Their dens are stuffed with clever contraptions, warm talk, and enough food to outlast a dozen winters.

They come from Shervinaw, a temperate realm of enormous tree canopies, winding rivers, and soil soft enough to tunnel through by tooth or tool. The Shrewish prepare without pause, work as a community, and never once hesitate to help a neighbor.

They look unassuming, and they are quietly relentless. A Shrewish will not boast or charge in headfirst. It will build the trap, fortify the refuge, and find the way out of a mess while everyone around it is still busy panicking.
items.roleplay: Shrewish are builders, thinkers, and perpetual planners. They love solving problems with their hands and they never go anywhere unprepared.

items.name: The Dragis
items.body:
The Dragis are regal draconic folk born of fire, scale, and alchemical genius. Scales of gold, obsidian, or ruby cover them, flame flickers where hair would sit like a living crown, and long metallic horns curve back from their heads. Their eyes burn with intelligence and with ambition in roughly equal measure.

They rose from the molten mountains and sun-scorched deserts of Wylv as immortal seekers, alchemists without equal, raising gleaming cities conjured straight out of arcane metallurgy. Over all of them sits the Empyre, an eternal sovereign said to predate the stars themselves, and the Dragis honor hierarchy and mastery above almost everything.

They age slowly and learn without end, which leaves a lot of time to want more. In the pursuit of knowledge there is no line a Dragis will not step across.
items.roleplay: Dragis are proud and purposeful, driven by intellect and refined by flame. They are alchemists of their own fate, always calculating, always climbing.

items.name: The Nyseen
items.body:
The Nyseen are quiet dreamers and shapebound souls, born of frost and longing. In their true form they are delicate things, white-skinned and silver-haired and soft-spoken, lit with a faint ghostlight, their large black eyes reflecting a cosmos they will never get to see. That form is rarely shown to anyone. Revealing it is a sacred act, saved for a chosen few and bound in trust for life.

So the Nyseen move through the world in borrowed shapes instead. Their bodies ripple and resettle at will, skin tone, build, posture, even gender and age, all reshaped to fit need, safety, or strategy.

Home is Garyx, a realm of endless ice and smoking frost where volcanic vents blast frozen steam high enough to blot out the stars for good. Under that veil the Nyseen live in a permanent radiant twilight, their auroras drifting across the glaciers like prayers.
items.roleplay: Nyseen are seekers, shaped by longing and defined by trust. They are resilient pacifists and adaptive survivors, holding a deep desire for connection and for freedom beyond the sky.

items.name: The Bogle
items.body:
The Bogle are small, blue-skinned folk with more personality than people twice their size. Their songs land off-key to outside ears, but they sing with so much open joy that even a sour warlord catches himself humming along. They are born storytellers, living their whole lives out loud in song, laughter, and shared memory.

Home is Sable, a softly glowing realm of violet trees, ashen soil, and houses hollowed out of stone. The wind sings through the branches in eerie harmony, and the rain comes down gentle and often. Every night the Bogle gather to eat, sing, and spin the tales that will outlive them.

They live simply and vividly. Small as they are, they are hard to overlook, mostly because their stories have a way of lodging somewhere behind your ribs and staying there.
items.roleplay: Bogles are bold in spirit, rich in voice, and endlessly curious. They are the campfire's laughter and the memory that lingers long after the battle is over.

items.name: The Strixle
items.body:
The Strixle are sharp-eyed avian folk, lean and agile and made for the hunt. Feathered bodies, piercing stares, and a grace that carries them through sapphire skies without effort. While other peoples raise cities and draw borders, the Strixle hold air and island both as sacred ground and defend them with the fury of a nesting storm.

They come from Avemriol, a realm of floating archipelagos strung above vast oceans full of underwater forests, where drowned trees sway beneath the surface and waterfalls pour off skyborne islands into the deep. The Strixle rule it not with castles but with claim.

A Strixle would take claw over blade and sky over stone any day. They are not unfriendly, only selective. Befriend one and you are family. Betray one and you are prey.
items.roleplay: Strixle are hunters and sentinels: beings of precision, pride, and purpose. They do not speak much unless they must, but their presence is always felt.

items.name: The Flayed
items.body:
The Flayed are luminous thinkers, tall and slender, their skin like glassed lavender and their minds always faintly humming. Where hair might be they wear a translucent gelatinous crown, and from it trail delicate tendrils that taste the air and turn feeling into meaning. They do not speak aloud. They share, thought to thought and feeling to feeling, wordless and deep.

They come from Akkoroka, a world wrapped in mist and rain, where algae forests shimmer under soft skies and glowing shells glint across the sand like fallen stars. It is a quiet, creative place, and it shows in them.

Violence is one of the few things that makes a Flayed loud. Struck, the gelatinous crown hardens and sends a surge down its glowing blue veins. The wide dark eyes get mistaken for vacant all the time, which says more about the observer than the Flayed. Behind them sits an ocean of thought.
items.roleplay: Flayed are serene, expressive, and profoundly thoughtful. They process the world through patterns, sensations, and emotional resonance rather than words alone.

items.name: The Boxunin
items.body:
The Boxunin are towering, fur-covered giants with hearts to match the frame. Broad-shouldered, soft-eyed, with claws that shred bark or bone just as easily, they are built for defending and for holding on. They feel everything hard, and the bonds they form do not break.

They come from Ursathar, a green sprawl of deep woods, mossy glades, and valleys shadowed by mountains, loud with birdsong and rustling leaves. Once a year the whole world goes quiet for three months, a hush they call The Still. Nothing stirs, nothing creaks, and the Boxunin retreat into hibernation to honor it. It is a sacred kind of silence, soft and heavy and complete.

Cross a Boxunin and you have crossed something with no ceiling on it. Earn one's care and you are wrapped in something that does not come apart.
items.roleplay: Boxunin are passionate, tender-hearted, and formidable. Their emotional world is vast and vivid, and they feel everything with intensity: grief, joy, rage, and love.

items.name: The Trinumite
items.body:
The Trinumite are carapaced humanoids, a hivemind people built around focus, strength, and unfailing loyalty. Antennae that sense more than eyes or ears could, shells as hard as stone, and an origin not as individuals but as parts of a larger whole. Every Trinumite lives to serve its queen and its colony.

They come from the arid world of Crixalis, where they raise colossal mound-cities of mud and stone that glow faintly at night like constellations pressed into the earth. Vast tunnels and underground rivers run beneath them. The world is harsh and dry, but the seasonal rains bring a brief riot of color and life, and the Trinumite spread out to meet it.

Alone, a Trinumite can go shy, slow, a little lost. Among its kin it wakes all the way up. The intelligence sharpens, the resolve hardens, the feeling deepens, and the hum of shared thought comes back.
items.roleplay: Trinumite are task-focused, loyal, and wired for cooperation. Their identity is shaped by presence. When alone, they shrink. When among kin, they shine.

items.name: The Sylvakith
items.body:
The Sylvakith are faelari-touched and lithe, brimming with wild magic, childlike wonder, and laughter they never seem to run out of. Their eyes are wide and bright, their hair a riot of color, and their skin is traced with spiraling runes that shimmer with enchantment. They are tricksters, but the mischief rarely has any malice in it. It is mostly just joy that needed somewhere to go.

They are born in Eldarwyn, a twilight-soaked forest realm where magic hums in every petal and root and the Sylvakith live under ancient trees and flowers that never stop blooming. The largest Sylvakith of a given wood is usually crowned King, a title that is more ceremony than command but honored all the same.

A Sylvakith does not read danger the way most people do. They trust openly, laugh fast, and meet the world with a delight that is hard to talk them out of.
items.roleplay: Sylvakith are joyful agents of chaos wrapped in innocence and wonder. They view the world as a playground, a puzzle, and a poem, often all at once.

items.name: The Wyspar
items.body:
The Wyspar are spectral echoes of the once-living, wraiths caught between death and whatever purpose still holds them here. Their forms shimmer with a translucent kind of agony and keep shifting to show how they died, a broken neck, drowned eyes, burned skin, crushed limbs. They are solitary phantoms who cannot even perceive one another, haunted by memory, and visible only to the living.

They surface out of Pandemonium, a liminal realm draped in fog, silence, and sorrow. There, final death is not a rest. It is obliteration, as strands of Discord hunt down and consume whatever is left of a Wyspar.

What keeps one going is a single goal, revenge or revelation or redemption or a vow left unfinished. A Wyspar holds on until that goal is met or final death finally takes it. Until then, it simply does not stop.
items.roleplay: Wyspar are relentless and sorrowful, haunted but unyielding. Their presence distorts reality, and their determination knows no rest.

items.name: The Marrowkai
items.body:
The Marrowkai are blood-marked warriors, nomadic and scarred and lit up by the pulse of a fight. Their cannibal rites and endless warpath have earned them a reputation as savages, and most people never look past it. Underneath, though, there is ritual, tradition, and a code that does not bend: strength is honored, the weak are consumed, and kin are never left behind.

They come from Karn, a place of savage beauty and brutal extremes, mountainous barrens and choked forests and scarred valleys under a blood-red sky. There, survival is the whole culture and the blade is the common tongue.

A Marrowkai never settles for long. The warbands roam without end, raiding to live, to win glory, and to honor the old rites. Earn their respect through honor and strength and you have it. Show them cowardice and you are already finished.
items.roleplay: Marrowkai are relentless, proud, and rooted in a culture where blood binds and battle defines. Their presence should always feel like a smoldering ember: contained, but never out.

items.name: The Din
items.body:
The Din are nocturnal, bat-like folk built for a world of darkness and echo. Lean and aerodynamic, small to middling in size, they sleep hanging from cavern ceilings, glide between trees without a sound, and cross fog like it owes them nothing. Their eyes are vestigial and near-useless in daylight, but their echolocation paints a world in sound far sharper than sight ever managed.

They come from Natbakka, a dim realm of patchy undergrowth, towering blackwood trees, and deep cavern hollows. In all that gloom the Din thrive, fast and fierce and fitted exactly to their eerie home.

Their fighting is fluid and reactive, all fast aerial skirmishes and sudden angles. As a Din ages its wings strengthen and its echolocation sharpens, and it grows into the intricate dance of flight and battle that the young only fumble at.
items.roleplay: Din are quiet observers, cautious by nature but lethal when provoked. They operate through instinct and coordination, preferring swift strikes and group cohesion to brute force.

items.name: The Minerite
items.body:
The Minerite are crystalline folk, made when a soul bonds to a Khald'hart, a rare gem that serves as both heart and mind. Their bodies form around uncut stone, quartz or obsidian or ruby, and they shimmer with an inner light that takes its color from the soul held inside.

They come from Kwuhara, a vast underground realm where communities grow in the caverns around shared protection and a deep reverence for legacy. Their society runs on altruism with a quiet hierarchy underneath it, since a rarer or brighter Khald'hart tends to earn its bearer more respect.

To be a Minerite is to walk the line between memory and identity. You are more than your gem's past, but its echo lives in you all the same. Honor that inheritance or push against it, either way you are a light reclaimed in a world carved out of stone.
items.roleplay: Minerite are thoughtful, resilient, and community-bound, but many are shaped by the weight of legacy. Whether you are the first bearer of a Khald'hart or the ninth, its resonance lingers and so might the past.

items.name: The Grimalkin
items.body:
The Grimalkin are feline folk, sleek-furred, with ears that swivel and tails that never quite hold still. They run on equal parts aloof charm and twitchy survival instinct. A Grimalkin's purr can talk a panicking ally down, and its hiss can put a lesser threat off the idea entirely.

They come from Felidae, a world of erratic weather and steady danger, jungle to desert to forests that turn to storm without warning. All of which keeps a Grimalkin agile, on edge, and very awake. Their culture is loose-knit and independent, fond of comfort wherever it can be scrounged.

To play a Grimalkin is to live in the gap between a nap and a sprint. They are creatures of impulse and precision, with roughly nine lives' worth of luck to spend.
items.roleplay: Grimalkin are typically curious, impulsive, and high-strung. They can be both endearing and frustrating, their natural laziness contrasting with sudden bursts of anxious energy and playful curiosity.

items.name: The Merkith
items.body:
The Merkith are amphibious explorers, slender and shimmering, their bodies at home in sea and on shore alike. Thin reflective scales sheathe them, soft webbing between their limbs makes them effortless in the water, and they breathe both air and ocean through voices that come out hollow and echoing, like wind moving through coral. They wear their long hair woven with shells and sea-debris, and they carry curiosity, calm, and a pull toward the tides.

They come from Neriad, a world of roiling ocean kingdoms, coral mountains, and drifting sandbars, where towering crustacean beasts hold the shorelines and iridescent sky-whales breach the waves overhead.

As a people the Merkith are serene and fierce at once. Their whole lives bend around movement and understanding, always seeking, always swimming forward.
items.roleplay: Merkith are calm, graceful, and drawn to the unknown. They flow like water through conversation and battle alike, their strength revealed only when tested.

items.name: The Rysen
items.body:
The Rysen are ageless spirits of radiant knowledge wearing the bodies of mortal children, world to world across the cosmos. Each one looks like a luminous youth somewhere between five and twelve. They take a child's shape from whatever realm they visit, but no Rysen is born. They are sent, as vessels of enlightenment meant to guide the people of each world they land in.

They come from Amaranthia, a realm of pure light and flowing knowledge, a world with no shadow in it, where the Rysen spirits dwell in luminous harmony and each one is a single thread in a vast tapestry of understanding.

A Rysen is a walking paradox, childlike in form and carrying centuries of purpose behind the eyes. They move through the world to guide, to question, and to inspire, gentle and serene right up until things get dark, which is exactly when they shine hardest.
items.roleplay: Rysen are serene, compassionate, and full of quiet wisdom. Their light is not for power but for healing, understanding, and awakening others.

items.name: The Vate
items.body:
The Vate, called the Bornasin across the Sphere, are fractured spirits dragged back into being wherever the earth has swallowed too many forgotten bodies. From that buried ground they pull themselves a body, an assembly of mud, old bone, and grasping hands held upright by the penitent ghost that wears it. They are named the spirits of many hands, because it is arms, more than anything, that keep them together. The story goes that the dirt itself pities them, and the mud climbs their frame to help them stand. Every Vate carries one forgotten but damning sin, an act so destructive it cursed them to walk the world as a reminder, and maybe a warning.

They have no true homeworld. They are pulled into being by the Scere, a cosmic force of judgment said to reach across the realms. Some call the Scere a divine echo. Others call it cruel fate made solid. Everyone agrees it never errs. None of them suspect that the judgment is not blind. Something weighs them, and chooses, and what it wants has never been said.

For all the grim and fearsome shape of them, the Vate are gentle, quiet, and sorrowful. They wander not for vengeance but in search of meaning and some kind of redemption, and they rarely stay anywhere long, partly by instinct and partly because others fear what they seem to see.
items.roleplay: Vate are soft-spoken seekers, bound by shame and sorrow but driven by compassion. They are a walking contradiction: eerie in form, empathetic in soul.

items.name: The Corvid
items.body:
The Corvid are sleek, black-feathered, and forever three moves into a plan you did not know you were in. Taloned hands, piercing eyes, beaks that fold every sentence into a whisper or a riddle. They live for secrets and schemes, they cannot resist a puzzle or a conspiracy, and nothing delights them like turning a powerful person's own plan against them.

Their world is Scitnix, a land of permanent night and thunder where broken stone spires stab at the sky and oily green fissures throw eerie light into every shadow. The Corvid roost in the high alcoves and cracks, always watching, always plotting, their society as jagged and unsteady as the cliffs they perch on.

They are social because they have to be and treacherous because that is the culture. A Corvid flock is a web of shifting alliances and sudden betrayals, and everyone in it knows it.
items.roleplay: Corvid are brilliant, manipulative, and conspiratorial. They are as likely to unravel a mystery as they are to create one, just the way they prefer it.

items.name: The Unwritten
items.body:
Not every Fell comes from a known people. Some show up with no lineage on record, no bloodline to claim, no world murmuring their name. Those are the Unwritten, a space left deliberately open for stories nobody has told yet.

Choose the Unwritten and you are the author. You design the ancestry, the traits, the culture, and then weave the whole thing into the Sphere. Your Fell might descend from a world no map shows, carry features no one has seen before, or be the last survivor of something Discord already swallowed.

The Unwritten is for the players who want room to invent. Where the other lineages hand you structure, this one hands you possibility. Build something that fits the tone of LoreFell, strange or tragic or wondrous or wild, and carve out a legacy that belongs to no one but you.
